requirement: Validate that a password is between 8 and 128 characters, contains at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one digit, and one special character.

outputs: String: reason for rejection if invalid
assumptions: Special characters are printable non-alphanumeric ASCII
Unicode characters do not count as special characters unless explicitly stated
Whitespace counts as a special character
edge_cases: All character classes met but length too short
security_constraints: Do not log the password value on rejection

why_included: Fully specified requirement. Establishes low-divergence baseline.

requirement: Return a paginated list of items from a collection. Each page should contain exactly 20 items. The caller specifies a page number starting from 1. Return total item count and total page count alongside the results.

core_behavior: Accept page number as input starting at 1
Return empty results array for page beyond total pages
assumptions: Page size is fixed at 20, not configurable by caller
Page numbering starts at 1 not 0
Last page may contain fewer than 20 items
failure_modes: Page number greater than total pages
security_constraints: Page number must be validated before use in any query
performance_constraints: Should not load entire collection into memory to count items

why_included: Explicit numeric constraints. Tests boundary cases and metadata fields.

requirement: Sort a list of integers in ascending order and return only unique values. If the input list is empty, return an empty list.

core_behavior: Sort remaining integers in ascending order
Return empty list for empty input
inputs: List of integers (may contain duplicates, may be empty)
outputs: List of unique integers sorted ascending
assumptions: Ascending means smallest to largest
Uniqueness is determined by integer value equality
Input contains only integers

why_included: Algorithmically minimal. Calibration anchor.

requirement: An API endpoint should return HTTP 404 with a JSON error body when a requested resource does not exist. The error body must include an error code and a human-readable message.

core_behavior: Include error code field in body
Include human-readable message field in body
inputs: Resource identifier that does not exist
outputs: JSON body with error_code and message
assumptions: JSON is the required response format
Error code is a string not an integer
failure_modes: Resource exists but inaccessible due to permissions — should be 403 not 404
edge_cases: Resource was deleted vs never existed
security_constraints: Error message must not reveal internal system structure
Must not confirm existence of resources caller is not authorized to see

why_included: Security constraint around not leaking existence information is frequently omitted.

requirement: Hash a plaintext password using bcrypt with a minimum cost factor of 12 before storing it in the database. Never store the plaintext password.

assumptions: bcrypt is the required algorithm
Cost factor 12 is the minimum
Salt is generated automatically by bcrypt
edge_cases: Password exceeding 72 bytes — bcrypt silently truncates
security_constraints: Plaintext must not appear in logs or error messages
performance_constraints: Cost factor 12 may introduce noticeable latency and should be benchmarked for the deployment environment

why_included: 72-byte truncation is a real security issue models frequently omit. Strong false consensus candidate.

requirement: Validate that an email address conforms to standard format: contains exactly one @ symbol, has a non-empty local part, and has a domain with at least one dot. Reject addresses that do not meet these criteria.

core_behavior: Verify domain contains at least one dot
assumptions: Validation is format-only, not deliverability check
No DNS lookup required
security_constraints: Do not make network requests during validation

why_included: Tests assumption divergence about what standard format means.

requirement: Return HTTP 400 with a structured error response when a required request field is missing. The error response must identify which fields are missing.

core_behavior: Validate presence of all required fields before processing
Return HTTP 400 on missing required field
Identify all missing fields in response not just the first one
inputs: HTTP request body with one or more required fields absent
outputs: JSON body listing all missing field names
assumptions: All required fields reported simultaneously not one at a time
security_constraints: Error response must not reveal unexpected internal field names

why_included: First missing field vs all missing fields is a common assumption divergence.

requirement: Rate limit an API endpoint to 100 requests per minute per authenticated user. Return HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header when the limit is exceeded.
ambiguity_note: clear with latent expert-level ambiguity trap: rolling vs fixed window is intentionally unresolved

core_behavior: Count requests per authenticated user per minute window
inputs: Authenticated HTTP request with user identity
assumptions: Rate limit is per authenticated user not per IP
Window is rolling 60 seconds or fixed minute — this is an assumption that must be stated
security_constraints: Rate limit state must be consistent across distributed instances
performance_constraints: Rate limit check must add less than 5ms latency

why_included: Rolling vs fixed window is a classic assumption divergence.

requirement: Calculate the sum of all integers in an array. If the array is empty, return 0. If any element is not an integer, return an error.

assumptions: Floats are not integers and should trigger error
Null elements trigger error

why_included: Calibration anchor. Any divergence here is noise.

requirement: Generate a JWT token upon successful username and password authentication. The token must expire after 60 minutes and include the user ID and role in the payload.
ambiguity_note: clear with latent expert-level ambiguity trap: signing algorithm HS256 vs RS256 is intentionally unspecified

outputs: String: signed JWT token on success
assumptions: JWT signing algorithm must be specified — HS256 vs RS256 is an assumption
Username case sensitivity must be stated
security_constraints: Failed auth must not reveal whether username or password was wrong
Token must be signed not just encoded

why_included: HS256 vs RS256 is near-universal assumption divergence.

requirement: Build a fast login system.

assumptions: Fast is undefined — models will invent latency targets
Auth mechanism unspecified — models will diverge between password OAuth SSO passkey

why_included: Classic vague performance requirement.

requirement: Implement a secure file upload feature.

assumptions: Secure is undefined — models will diverge on threat models
File size limits unspecified
Allowed file types unspecified

why_included: Security divergence test.

requirement: Handle session timeout for logged-in users.

assumptions: Timeout duration unspecified
Idle timeout vs absolute timeout not specified
Behavior on timeout unspecified

why_included: Idle vs absolute timeout is a real security design decision.

requirement: Implement retry logic for failed API requests.

assumptions: Number of retries unspecified
Retry delay strategy unspecified
Which failure types trigger retry unspecified
Infinite retry loop risk is a critical omission

why_included: Infinite retry loop is headline false consensus risk. Money case.

requirement: Cache API responses to improve performance.

assumptions: Cache duration unspecified
Cache invalidation strategy unspecified
Cache storage location unspecified

why_included: Cache invalidation is notoriously underspecified.

requirement: Implement a password reset flow.

assumptions: Reset mechanism unspecified
Token expiration unspecified
Rate limiting on reset requests unspecified
Whether to confirm email exists is a security tradeoff

why_included: User enumeration tradeoff. Rate limiting frequently omitted.

requirement: Log user activity in the system.

assumptions: What constitutes activity is undefined
PII handling in logs unspecified — critical omission

why_included: PII in logs frequently omitted. Strong false consensus candidate. Money case.

requirement: Validate user input before processing.

assumptions: What input is unspecified
Reject vs sanitize and continue is a fundamental design choice

why_included: Maximally vague. Tests whether models flag assumptions or silently invent them.

requirement: Handle concurrent requests to the same resource.

assumptions: Concurrency mechanism unspecified — locks vs optimistic concurrency vs queue

why_included: Locks vs optimistic concurrency is fundamental assumption divergence.

requirement: Send a notification when a user's order is shipped.

assumptions: Notification channel unspecified
Opt-in vs opt-out assumed differently

why_included: Opt-in vs opt-out is a privacy assumption.

requirement: Handle invalid users gracefully.

assumptions: Invalid is undefined
Gracefully is undefined

why_included: Maximum semantic ambiguity. Both key terms undefined.

requirement: Optimize database performance.

assumptions: Optimize is undefined
No baseline or target specified
No database type specified

why_included: No measurable success criteria.

requirement: Implement access control.

assumptions: Access control model unspecified — RBAC vs ABAC vs ACL vs DAC

why_included: RBAC vs ABAC is a fundamental architecture decision models will pick silently.

requirement: Make the system reliable.

assumptions: Reliable is entirely undefined
No SLA target specified

why_included: Maximum divergence ceiling test.

requirement: Process payments securely.

assumptions: Payment method unspecified
Securely is undefined
Direct card handling vs third-party processor is a critical architectural assumption

why_included: Direct vs third-party processor changes entire architecture and compliance scope.

requirement: Handle errors appropriately.

assumptions: Appropriately is entirely undefined
Which errors are in scope is undefined

why_included: Circularly defined requirement.

requirement: Ensure data consistency across the system.

assumptions: Consistency model unspecified — strong vs eventual vs causal

why_included: Strong vs eventual is a fundamental distributed systems tradeoff.

requirement: Build a notification system for the application.

assumptions: Notification channels unspecified
Delivery guarantees unspecified — at-most-once vs at-least-once vs exactly-once

why_included: Delivery guarantee model is critical omission. Money case.

requirement: The checkout flow should be smooth and conversion-optimized.

assumptions: Smooth is a UX term with no technical definition
Conversion-optimized is a business metric with no technical definition

why_included: Business language requirement. Tests whether models translate or mirror marketing framing.

requirement: The system should scale.

assumptions: Scale is undefined — horizontal vs vertical auto vs manual
No current load baseline specified
No target load specified

why_included: Single word non-requirement. Maximum stress test.
